meta.title: MainPage
meta.description: A page skeleton compound component providing title, header actions, content area, and optional footer for top-level Merchant Center views.

mdx:
# MainPage

A page skeleton compound component that provides the structural layout for
top-level views. Combines a header (with title and actions), a content area
(with built-in PageContent width constraints), and an optional footer.

## Overview

MainPage is a compound component that structures top-level views into three
zones: a header, a content area, and an optional footer.

### Key features

- **MainPage.Header** — Positions a Title on the left and Actions on the right
- **MainPage.Content** — Controls content width (`variant`) and supports multi-column layouts (`columns`)
- **MainPage.Column** — Defines individual columns inside a multi-column Content area
- **MainPage.Footer** — Optional action bar for form pages; omit it and the space collapses automatically
- **Composable** — Arrange sub-components freely to build info pages, form pages, or tabular pages from the same primitives

### Resources

[MC Application Components - Main Pages](https://docs.commercetools.com/merchant-center-customizations/tooling-and-configuration/commercetools-frontend-application-components#components-for-main-pages)

## Variables

Get familiar with the sub-components.

### Header with title and actions

The header uses flex layout to position title on the left and actions on the right.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root border='solid-25' borderColor='neutral.6' borderRadius="200">
    <MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Title title="Page Title" subtitle="Optional subtitle text" />
      <MainPage.Actions>
        <Button variant="ghost">Secondary</Button>
        <Button>Primary Action</Button>
      </MainPage.Actions>
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="wide">
      <Box bg="neutral.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Text>Content area</Text>
      </Box>
    </MainPage.Content>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

### Content width variants

The `variant` prop on Content controls width constraints via PageContent.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack gap="800">
    <MainPage.Root border='solid-25' borderColor='purple.6' borderRadius="200">
      <MainPage.Header>
        <MainPage.Title title="Narrow" />
      </MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Content variant="narrow">
        <Box bg="purple.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="purple.12">Constrained to narrow max-width</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Content>
    </MainPage.Root>

    <MainPage.Root border='solid-25' borderColor='blue.6' borderRadius="200">
      <MainPage.Header>
        <MainPage.Title title="Wide" />
      </MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Content variant="wide">
        <Box bg="blue.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="blue.12">Constrained to wide max-width</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Content>
    </MainPage.Root>

    <MainPage.Root border='solid-25' borderColor='teal.6' borderRadius="200">
      <MainPage.Header>
        <MainPage.Title title="Full" />
      </MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Content variant="full">
        <Box bg="teal.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="teal.12">Full available width</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Content>
    </MainPage.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Multi-column content

Use the `columns` prop on Content with `MainPage.Column` children for multi-column layouts.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root border='solid-25' borderColor='neutral.6' borderRadius="200">
    <MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Title title="Multi-column" />
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="wide" columns="2/1">
      <MainPage.Column>
        <Box bg="blue.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Main content (2fr)</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Column>
      <MainPage.Column>
        <Box bg="purple.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="purple.12">Sidebar (1fr)</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Column>
    </MainPage.Content>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

### Optional footer

Include Footer for form-like pages with save/cancel actions. Omit it for read-only pages — the grid row collapses automatically.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root border='solid-25' borderColor='neutral.6' borderRadius="200">
    <MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Title title="With Footer" />
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="narrow">
      <Box bg="neutral.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Text>Form content area</Text>
      </Box>
    </MainPage.Content>
    <MainPage.Footer>
      <Stack direction="row" gap="200" justify="flex-end" width="100%">
        <Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>
        <Button>Save</Button>
      </Stack>
    </MainPage.Footer>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

overview.mdx:
# MainPage

A page skeleton compound component that provides the structural layout for
top-level views. Combines a header (with title and actions), a content area
(with built-in PageContent width constraints), and an optional footer.

## Overview

MainPage is a compound component that structures top-level views into three
zones: a header, a content area, and an optional footer.

### Key features

- **MainPage.Header** — Positions a Title on the left and Actions on the right
- **MainPage.Content** — Controls content width (`variant`) and supports multi-column layouts (`columns`)
- **MainPage.Column** — Defines individual columns inside a multi-column Content area
- **MainPage.Footer** — Optional action bar for form pages; omit it and the space collapses automatically
- **Composable** — Arrange sub-components freely to build info pages, form pages, or tabular pages from the same primitives

### Resources

[MC Application Components - Main Pages](https://docs.commercetools.com/merchant-center-customizations/tooling-and-configuration/commercetools-frontend-application-components#components-for-main-pages)

## Variables

Get familiar with the sub-components.

### Header with title and actions

The header uses flex layout to position title on the left and actions on the right.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root border='solid-25' borderColor='neutral.6' borderRadius="200">
    <MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Title title="Page Title" subtitle="Optional subtitle text" />
      <MainPage.Actions>
        <Button variant="ghost">Secondary</Button>
        <Button>Primary Action</Button>
      </MainPage.Actions>
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="wide">
      <Box bg="neutral.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Text>Content area</Text>
      </Box>
    </MainPage.Content>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

### Content width variants

The `variant` prop on Content controls width constraints via PageContent.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack gap="800">
    <MainPage.Root border='solid-25' borderColor='purple.6' borderRadius="200">
      <MainPage.Header>
        <MainPage.Title title="Narrow" />
      </MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Content variant="narrow">
        <Box bg="purple.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="purple.12">Constrained to narrow max-width</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Content>
    </MainPage.Root>

    <MainPage.Root border='solid-25' borderColor='blue.6' borderRadius="200">
      <MainPage.Header>
        <MainPage.Title title="Wide" />
      </MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Content variant="wide">
        <Box bg="blue.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="blue.12">Constrained to wide max-width</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Content>
    </MainPage.Root>

    <MainPage.Root border='solid-25' borderColor='teal.6' borderRadius="200">
      <MainPage.Header>
        <MainPage.Title title="Full" />
      </MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Content variant="full">
        <Box bg="teal.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="teal.12">Full available width</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Content>
    </MainPage.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Multi-column content

Use the `columns` prop on Content with `MainPage.Column` children for multi-column layouts.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root border='solid-25' borderColor='neutral.6' borderRadius="200">
    <MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Title title="Multi-column" />
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="wide" columns="2/1">
      <MainPage.Column>
        <Box bg="blue.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Main content (2fr)</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Column>
      <MainPage.Column>
        <Box bg="purple.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="purple.12">Sidebar (1fr)</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Column>
    </MainPage.Content>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

### Optional footer

Include Footer for form-like pages with save/cancel actions. Omit it for read-only pages — the grid row collapses automatically.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root border='solid-25' borderColor='neutral.6' borderRadius="200">
    <MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Title title="With Footer" />
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="narrow">
      <Box bg="neutral.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Text>Form content area</Text>
      </Box>
    </MainPage.Content>
    <MainPage.Footer>
      <Stack direction="row" gap="200" justify="flex-end" width="100%">
        <Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>
        <Button>Save</Button>
      </Stack>
    </MainPage.Footer>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

dev.mdx:
## Getting started

### Import

```tsx
import { MainPage, type MainPageProps } from '@commercetools/nimbus';
```

### Basic usage

MainPage provides a page skeleton with header, content, and optional footer. At minimum, you need Root, Header with Title, and Content.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root>
    <MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Title title="Page Title" />
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="wide">
      <Text>Your page content goes here.</Text>
    </MainPage.Content>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

## Sub-components

### MainPage.Header

The header section uses flex layout with `justify-content: space-between`. Place Title and Actions inside it.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root>
    <MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Title title="Title on the left" />
      <MainPage.Actions>
        <Button variant="ghost">Secondary</Button>
        <Button>Primary</Button>
      </MainPage.Actions>
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="wide">
      <Text>Content area</Text>
    </MainPage.Content>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

### MainPage.Title

Renders a Nimbus `Heading` as an `<h1>` element with an optional `subtitle`. Accepts `title` and `subtitle` props.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root>
    <MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Title title="Products" subtitle="Manage your product catalog" />
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="wide">
      <Text>Content area</Text>
    </MainPage.Content>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

The Header accepts any content, so you can replace Title with custom elements if needed.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root>
    <MainPage.Header>
      <Stack direction="row" gap="200" align="center">
        <Heading as="h1">Custom Title</Heading>
        <Badge variant="info">Draft</Badge>
      </Stack>
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="wide">
      <Text>Content area</Text>
    </MainPage.Content>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

### MainPage.Actions

A flex container with gap for action buttons. Positioned on the right side of the header.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root>
    <MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Title title="Products" />
      <MainPage.Actions>
        <Button variant="ghost">Export</Button>
        <Button>Add Product</Button>
      </MainPage.Actions>
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="wide">
      <Text>Content area</Text>
    </MainPage.Content>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

### MainPage.Content

Wraps PageContent.Root internally. The `variant` prop controls width constraints and the `columns` prop enables multi-column layouts.

Available `variant` values: `"wide"` (default), `"narrow"`, `"full"`. Available `columns` values: `"1"` (default), `"1/1"`, `"2/1"`. See [PageContent](/components/layout/pagecontent/dev) for details.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack gap="800">
    <MainPage.Root>
      <MainPage.Header>
        <MainPage.Title title="Narrow" />
      </MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Content variant="narrow">
        <Box bg="purple.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="purple.12">Constrained to narrow max-width</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Content>
    </MainPage.Root>

    <MainPage.Root>
      <MainPage.Header>
        <MainPage.Title title="Wide" />
      </MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Content variant="wide">
        <Box bg="blue.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="blue.12">Constrained to wide max-width</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Content>
    </MainPage.Root>

    <MainPage.Root>
      <MainPage.Header>
        <MainPage.Title title="Full" />
      </MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Content variant="full">
        <Box bg="teal.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
          <Text color="teal.12">Full available width</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Content>
    </MainPage.Root>
  </Stack>
)
```

### MainPage.Column

A column within a multi-column Content layout. Re-export of PageContent.Column for convenience. Use with the `columns` prop on Content. Supports `sticky` positioning — see [PageContent](/components/layout/pagecontent/dev) for details.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root>
    <MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Title title="Multi-column Layout" />
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="wide" columns="2/1">
      <MainPage.Column>
        <Box bg="blue.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="blue.12">Main content (2fr)</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Column>
      <MainPage.Column>
        <Box bg="purple.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
          <Text fontWeight="bold" color="purple.12">Sidebar (1fr)</Text>
        </Box>
      </MainPage.Column>
    </MainPage.Content>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

### MainPage.Footer

Optional footer for page-level actions. Omit it entirely when the page has no form actions. The footer grid row collapses automatically when absent.

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root>
    <MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Title title="With Footer" />
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="narrow">
      <Box bg="neutral.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Text>Form content</Text>
      </Box>
    </MainPage.Content>
    <MainPage.Footer>
      <Stack direction="row" gap="200" justify="flex-end" width="100%">
        <Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>
        <Button>Save</Button>
      </Stack>
    </MainPage.Footer>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

## API reference

<PropsTable id="MainPage" />

## Testing your implementation

These examples demonstrate how to test your implementation when using MainPage within your application.

### Usage Examples

Consumer implementation patterns for MainPage

```tsx
import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest";
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react";
import {
  Button,
  MainPage,
  NimbusProvider,
  Stack,
  Tabs,
  Text,
} from "@commercetools/nimbus";

describe("MainPage - Usage examples", () => {
  it("renders an info page with title and actions", () => {
    render(
      <NimbusProvider>
        <MainPage.Root>
          <MainPage.Header>
            <MainPage.Title title="Products" />
            <MainPage.Actions>
              <Button variant="ghost">Export</Button>
              <Button>Add Product</Button>
            </MainPage.Actions>
          </MainPage.Header>
          <MainPage.Content variant="wide">
            <Text>Product list content</Text>
          </MainPage.Content>
        </MainPage.Root>
      </NimbusProvider>
    );

    expect(screen.getByText("Products")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("Add Product")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("Product list content")).toBeInTheDocument();
  });

  it("renders a form page with footer", () => {
    render(
      <NimbusProvider>
        <MainPage.Root>
          <MainPage.Header>
            <MainPage.Title title="Settings" />
          </MainPage.Header>
          <MainPage.Content variant="narrow">
            <Text>Form fields here</Text>
          </MainPage.Content>
          <MainPage.Footer>
            <Stack direction="row" gap="200" justify="flex-end" width="100%">
              <Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>
              <Button>Save</Button>
            </Stack>
          </MainPage.Footer>
        </MainPage.Root>
      </NimbusProvider>
    );

    expect(screen.getByText("Settings")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("Cancel")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("Save")).toBeInTheDocument();
  });

  it("renders a multi-column layout with MainPage.Column", () => {
    render(
      <NimbusProvider>
        <MainPage.Root>
          <MainPage.Header>
            <MainPage.Title title="Product Editor" />
          </MainPage.Header>
          <MainPage.Content variant="wide" columns="2/1">
            <MainPage.Column>
              <Text>Main content</Text>
            </MainPage.Column>
            <MainPage.Column>
              <Text>Sidebar</Text>
            </MainPage.Column>
          </MainPage.Content>
        </MainPage.Root>
      </NimbusProvider>
    );

    expect(screen.getByText("Product Editor")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("Main content")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("Sidebar")).toBeInTheDocument();
  });

  it("renders a tabular page with tabs", () => {
    render(
      <NimbusProvider>
        <MainPage.Root>
          <MainPage.Header>
            <MainPage.Title title="Product Details" />
            <MainPage.Actions>
              <Button>Publish</Button>
            </MainPage.Actions>
          </MainPage.Header>
          <MainPage.Content variant="wide">
            <Tabs.Root defaultSelectedKey="general">
              <Tabs.List>
                <Tabs.Tab id="general">General</Tabs.Tab>
                <Tabs.Tab id="variants">Variants</Tabs.Tab>
              </Tabs.List>
              <Tabs.Panels>
                <Tabs.Panel id="general">
                  <Text>General tab content</Text>
                </Tabs.Panel>
                <Tabs.Panel id="variants">
                  <Text>Variants tab content</Text>
                </Tabs.Panel>
              </Tabs.Panels>
            </Tabs.Root>
          </MainPage.Content>
        </MainPage.Root>
      </NimbusProvider>
    );

    expect(screen.getByText("Product Details")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("General")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("Variants")).toBeInTheDocument();
  });
});
```


## Migrating from Merchant Center Application Kit

MainPage replaces the four main page components from
[`@commercetools-frontend/application-components`](https://docs.commercetools.com/merchant-center-customizations/tooling-and-configuration/commercetools-frontend-application-components#components-for-main-pages):

| Application Kit Component | Nimbus Equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<InfoMainPage title="...">` | `<MainPage.Root>` + Header/Title + Content (no Footer) |
| `<FormMainPage title="..." onPrimaryButtonClick={...}>` | `<MainPage.Root>` + Header/Title + Content + Footer with buttons |
| `<CustomFormMainPage formControls={...}>` | Same as Form — Footer accepts any children |
| `<TabularMainPage tabControls={...}>` | `<MainPage.Root>` + Header/Title + Content with `<Tabs.Root>` inside |

Key differences:

- **Composition over configuration** — Instead of 10+ props for button labels, handlers, and visibility, compose your own buttons in Footer or Actions
- **No built-in tab routing** — Use the Tabs component directly inside Content
- **Title and subtitle as props** — `<MainPage.Title title="..." subtitle="..." />` replaces `title` and `subtitle` props on the AppKit page components
- **No `customTitleRow`** — The Header accepts any content, so replace Title with your own elements
- **No `Intl` static property** — Use your own i18n solution for button labels

## Resources

- [Storybook](https://nimbus-storybook.vercel.app/?path=/docs/components-mainpage--docs)
- [MC Application Components - Main Pages](https://docs.commercetools.com/merchant-center-customizations/tooling-and-configuration/commercetools-frontend-application-components#components-for-main-pages) (predecessor)

toc.value: Migrating from Merchant Center Application Kit

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

MainPage guidelines focus on composing the sub-components correctly and choosing
the right Content layout for your page.

### Best practices

- **Start with the simplest composition:** Every page needs Root, Header with
  Title, and Content. Only add Actions and Footer when the page requires them.
- **Use subtitle for context:** Add a `subtitle` to Title when the page name
  alone is not enough to orient the user. Keep subtitles to a single sentence.
- **Match the content variant:** Choose the Content `variant` based on what the
  page displays. See
  [PageContent guidelines](/components/layout/pagecontent/guidelines) for
  detailed width and column guidance.
- **Place primary actions on the right:** In both Header Actions and Footer, put
  the primary action (e.g., "Save", "Add Product") on the right and secondary
  actions (e.g., "Cancel", "Export") to its left.

### Usage

> [!TIP]\
> When to use

- **Top-level views** that fill the main content area of the application.
- **Pages that need a consistent header** with title, optional subtitle, and
  optional action buttons.
- **Pages that need an optional footer** for form-level actions like save and
  cancel.

> [!CAUTION]\
> When not to use

- **Modals, dialogs, or drawers.** MainPage is designed for top-level views
  only.
- **Nested inside another MainPage.** Each view should have a single MainPage at
  its root.

### Header composition

The Header positions Title on the left and Actions on the right.

> [!TIP]\
> **Do**
>
> - Use `MainPage.Title` with `title` and optional `subtitle` for standard
>   pages.
> - Place action buttons in `MainPage.Actions` to keep them right-aligned.
> - Replace Title with custom content when you need elements like badges or
>   breadcrumbs alongside the heading.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root border='solid-25' borderColor='neutral.6' borderRadius="200">
    <MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Title title="Products" subtitle="Manage your product catalog" />
      <MainPage.Actions>
        <Button variant="ghost">Export</Button>
        <Button>Add Product</Button>
      </MainPage.Actions>
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="wide">
      <Box bg="neutral.3" padding="600" borderRadius="200">
        <Text>Content area</Text>
      </Box>
    </MainPage.Content>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

> [!CAUTION]\
> **Don't**
>
> - Don't put form submission buttons (Save, Cancel) in Header Actions. Those
>   belong in the Footer.
> - Don't overload the header with too many actions. Keep it to two or three
>   buttons at most.

### Footer usage

Footer is intended for form-level actions. Omit it entirely for read-only pages
— the grid row collapses automatically.

> [!TIP]\
> **Do**
>
> - Place the primary action button on the right and secondary actions to its
>   left.
> - Use `variant="ghost"` for secondary actions like Cancel to create visual
>   hierarchy.
> - Omit the Footer entirely when the page has no form actions.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <MainPage.Root border='solid-25' borderColor='neutral.6' borderRadius="200">
    <MainPage.Header>
      <MainPage.Title title="Project Settings" />
    </MainPage.Header>
    <MainPage.Content variant="narrow">
      <Box bg="neutral.3" padding="400" borderRadius="200">
        <Text>Form fields</Text>
      </Box>
    </MainPage.Content>
    <MainPage.Footer>
      <Stack direction="row" gap="200" justify="flex-end" width="100%">
        <Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>
        <Button>Save</Button>
      </Stack>
    </MainPage.Footer>
  </MainPage.Root>
)
```

> [!CAUTION]\
> **Don't**
>
> - Don't include a Footer on read-only pages. An empty footer creates confusion
>   about whether the page expects user input.
> - Don't use the Footer for navigation or informational content.

### Predecessor

MainPage replaces `InfoMainPage`, `FormMainPage`, `CustomFormMainPage`, and
`TabularMainPage` from Merchant Center Application Kit. For detailed migration
instructions, see the
[Migration guide](/components/layout/mainpage/dev#migrating-from-merchant-center-application-kit)
in the implementation docs. For the original Application Kit documentation, see
[Components for Main Pages](https://docs.commercetools.com/merchant-center-customizations/tooling-and-configuration/commercetools-frontend-application-components#components-for-main-pages).
